response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine breadth — high-throughput distributed systems at Sociomantic, AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, and consistent engineering craftsmanship across a long career. However, Python is entirely absent from the resume despite being a must-have skill, the most relevant distributed-systems work is from 2018–2020, the AI exposure at re:cinq lasted only six months before a current career gap, and there is limited evidence of the cross-functional stakeholder management and operational ownership this staff-level role demands. The profile is interesting enough to warrant a screening call to probe the Python gap and depth of AI-native experience, but as-written it falls short of the bar.
strengths: Hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework work at re:cinq directly maps to the AI-native engineering environment the role expects
Demonstrated high-throughput, low-latency distributed-systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Long track record of engineering craftsmanship — TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, design patterns — across many languages and domains
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume despite being listed as a must-have; TypeScript is present but Python fluency is unverified
Active career gap since October 2025 and only six months at the most recent (most relevant) role raises questions about continuity and depth of AI-native exposure
No explicit evidence of staff-level cross-functional stakeholder management, on-call operational ownership, or incident-response capability
